subject: Human Resources
title: Digital Nomads and Location Independence
description: One-minute skill: Digital Nomads and Location Independence.

hook.text: A software engineer works from Bali on Monday, Lisbon on Thursday. Their employer is in San Francisco.

buildup.text: Digital nomads work remotely while traveling—enabled by reliable internet, collaboration tools, and companies that hire globally.

discovery.text: An estimated 35 million people worldwide identified as digital nomads by 2023—a number growing 50% year over year.

twist.text: HR nightmares multiply: tax laws, employment regulations, data privacy, and benefits eligibility shift with every border.

climax.text: Countries now offer digital nomad visas—Portugal, Estonia, Barbados—competing for remote workers and economic activity they bring.

punchline.text: Work used to happen where you lived. Now you live where you want and work from anywhere.

quiz.question: What HR challenge do digital nomads create?
options: Tax, employment law, and data privacy vary across jurisdictions
They are more productive than office workers
